scope_summary: Consolidated state-wide water policy ensuring planning and development considers all water matters: environmental/social/cultural values of water resources, riverine flooding, water infrastructure and supply (wastewater, stormwater, groundwater), public drinking water source areas, and specific measures for the Peel-Harvey coastal plain catchment and Swan Canning river system. It repeals SPP 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.7, 2.9 Water Resources and 2.10 (and the draft SPP 2.9 Planning for Water) and replaces Better Urban Water Management and the Government Sewerage Policy. Applied with its policy mapping and Water Guidelines to planning instruments, subdivision and development proposals.

key_numeric_standards.unit: m above the 1% AEP flood event
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: habitable development in flood-prone areas dominated by riverine processes (or alternative height per an endorsed floodplain development strategy)
land draining to and within 2 km of listed inlets, estuarine areas and coastal embayments (e.g. Cockburn Sound, Geographe Bay)

key_numeric_standards.topic: sensitive water resource area extent (other estuarine areas)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: land draining to and within 1 km of other estuarine areas, except port-approved portions

key_numeric_standards.topic: sensitive water resource area extent (significant wetlands)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: land near significant wetlands (1 km where groundwater gradient unknown or variable)

quality_flags: manifest instrument_name uses the draft-era title 'Planning for Water'; the gazetted final policy is titled 'State Planning Policy 2.9 Water' - same instrument, title should be updated
two-column PDF extraction interleaves columns in places (some clause text merged across columns)

very recent instrument (operational 18 December 2025) - it repealed six other SPPs, so any manifest or alias entries pointing at SPP 2.1/2.2/2.3/2.7/2.10 or the old SPP 2.9 Water Resources are now superseded
